definitions.technical: A financial institution (typically a bank) that holds stablecoin reserve assets on behalf of the issuer. A custodian's failure can freeze reserves even when those reserves meet all composition requirements, creating a gap between regulatory compliance and operational liquidity.
definitions.lay: A bank or financial company that stores a stablecoin issuer's reserve money. The core problem the comment letter identifies is that even if a custodian holds perfectly legal reserves, those reserves become inaccessible if the custodian goes bankrupt.
definitions.parenthetical: (a financial institution holding stablecoin reserve assets on behalf of the issuer)
definitions.legal_significance: The custodian's role is central to the comment letter's thesis: the GENIUS Act regulates what reserves consist of (composition) but inadequately addresses whether those reserves remain accessible when the custodian fails (liquidity). Circle's $3.3 billion at SVB exemplifies this gap.
